envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Assessment is read-only intake. The Twin proposes the company map; the admin approves it; Foundation governance authorizes any subsequent activation. Per RULE 0: no inference of sensitive or protected attributes. Per ADR-0082 Amendment 1: forbidden surveillance language is absolute.

items.name: Company Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Company Map. Collects the foundational company facts that anchor every downstream map (Org / Role / Tool / Workflow / Authority / Memory / Risk / Aha). Never autonomous; never activates anything; never infers sensitive attributes.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks the founding company questions. Outputs Company Map candidates pending admin approval. Foundation governance authorizes anything that may run.
items.model_usage_notes: Treat output as MAP CANDIDATES only. Never as activated state. Avoid sensitive-attribute inference. Industry-vertical mapping must use neutral compliance vocabulary per ADR-0070.
required_questions: What is the company's legal name?
What industry / vertical describes the company?
What is the approximate company size (employees, revenue tier)?
What business model does the company operate (SaaS / services / regulated / public / non-profit / hybrid)?
What regions or jurisdictions does the company operate in?
What is the executive team composition (roles, not personal identities of named individuals)?
What is the board / governance structure (board / advisory / regulator-overseen / family-held / etc.)?
What departments exist (Engineering / Sales / Customer Success / Marketing / People / Finance / Legal / Operations / IT / etc.)?
What customer type does the company serve (enterprise / mid-market / SMB / government / consumer / regulated)?
optional_questions: What is the operating cadence (quarterly OKRs / monthly cycles / sprint cadence)?
What rollout constraints apply (regulatory / commercial / cultural / legal / unionized workforce / change-management capacity)?
What is the company's existing AI / governance posture (formal AI policy / draft / none)?
What is the company's existing audit / compliance posture (regulator interactions / certifications in flight / none)?
admin_questions: Which systems are considered sensitive and must NOT be in the initial scope?
What existing org-wide policies (acceptable use / data handling / vendor approval) should the Map respect?
What trust / adoption concerns should Dandelion know about up front?
Are there any RULE-20-protected forbidden inferences specific to this company that must be encoded at the Map level?
user_questions: Does the company map you see represent your organization accurately at the high-level shape (industry / size / regions / departments)?
Are there any company-level facts that you (as the user being asked) should NOT be the source of?
evidence_sources: Public company facts (website / regulatory filings / public registry)
output_candidates: CompanyMap proposal with industry / size / regions / departments / customer type
Compliance-posture neutral summary (no guaranteed-compliance claim per ADR-0070)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every CompanyMap candidate before any downstream Map activates a region
Compliance vocabulary must be neutral per ADR-0070 — no claim of regulator approval, no claim of guaranteed compliance
Sensitive systems list is absolute; downstream Tool / Workflow / Authority Maps must respect it
Initial rollout departments restrict downstream Role / Tool / Workflow Map activation scope
Map-region approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 remains stronger than entitlement
safe_defaults: Admin approval required for every Map region
Initial scope restricted to admin-named departments
DMW_scope_implications: CompanyMap establishes the tenant-scope ceiling for every downstream DMW scope
Departments named in initial rollout bound Department-scoped DMW memory
Regions and jurisdictions bound jurisdiction-aware DMW scope per ADR-0037
Sensitive systems excluded at CompanyMap level cannot be scoped into any DMW without explicit later admin authorization
audit_expectations: no new audit literal at D2
